full_prompt: Design the load-bearing chassis plate of an autonomous mobile robot intended for the UK Robotics Challenge 2026. The robot must fit within an arena-size envelope of 450 mm length x 400 mm width x 350 mm height, weigh no more than 12 kg wet, and carry a 2 kg transport payload in a 150 x 150 x 100 mm interior compartment. The chassis plate is a single flat laser-cut / CNC-routed sheet of 6061-T6 aluminum (E 68.9 GPa, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), 3 mm nominal thickness, with four standard skid-steer wheel motor mounts (mass 400 g each, torque 2 Nm) at corners and a central compartment cutout. Robot accelerates up to 2 m/s on smooth floor and can traverse 20 mm height ramps. Load cases: (LC1) static gravity with 12 kg wet mass supported on four wheel-mount bolt patterns; (LC2) obstacle traversal: front wheels encounter 20 mm ramp at 2 m/s, reaction peak at front wheel mounts is 2 g equivalent vertical on 6 kg (front axle load) = 118 N per front wheel mount; (LC3) motor torque reaction at one skid-steer turn: each wheel sees 2 Nm motor torque, reacting at the motor-mount bolts as a couple; (LC4) payload drop inside compartment: 2 kg payload shifts 20 mm and hits the compartment wall at 2 m/s (conservative 5 g equivalent lateral on 2 kg at the compartment wall = 98 N lateral); (LC5) handling handle-lift: a user grabs the chassis at two diagonally opposed corner-handle cutouts and lifts the whole 12 kg robot - 60 N each handle upward; (LC6) drop from 300 mm: full robot 12 kg hits floor at 2.43 m/s, equivalent 10 g static deceleration distributed on chassis plate. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress under LC1-LC5 must remain below 184 MPa (276 / 1.5 yield FoS); (R2) under LC6 drop case, stress must remain below 276 MPa (yield, no permanent deformation); (R3) chassis plate dry mass must not exceed 1.2 kg; (R4) first bending natural frequency must exceed 50 Hz to decouple from drive-motor torque ripple; (R5) maximum plate deflection under LC1 static gravity must remain below 1 mm to preserve ground clearance of the central compartment. Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses not primary gates. Deliverables: STEP AP242 solid with mount holes and cutouts, FEA per load case, modal report, mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the load-bearing chassis plate of an autonomous mobile robot for the UK Robotics Challenge 2026. Arena envelope 450 x 400 x 350 mm, wet mass <= 12 kg, 2 kg transport payload in a 150 x 150 x 100 mm interior compartment. Peak speed 2 m/s, 20 mm ramp traversal.

Envelope: single flat laser-cut / CNC-routed 6061-T6 sheet, 3 mm nominal; four skid-steer wheel-motor mounts (400 g each, 2 N-m torque) at the corners; central compartment cutout; two diagonal corner-handle cutouts.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 on LC1-LC5 (allowable 184 MPa); LC6 drop allowed to full yield (276 MPa); plate dry mass <= 1.2 kg; first bending mode > 50 Hz; static gravity deflection < 1 mm.

Load cases.
- Static gravity: 12 kg supported at the four wheel-mount bolt patterns.
- Obstacle traversal: 20 mm ramp at 2 m/s = 2 g vertical on 6 kg front axle (118 N per front mount).
- Motor torque reaction at skid-steer turn: 2 N-m at each wheel as a couple at the motor-mount bolts.
- In-compartment payload shift: 2 kg hits the wall at 2 m/s (5 g lateral = 98 N).
- Handle lift: 60 N upward at each of two diagonally opposed corner handles.
- Drop from 300 mm: 12 kg at 2.43 m/s = 10 g equivalent static distributed on the plate.

prompt.title: UK Robotics Challenge 2026 Mobile Robot Chassis Plate
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 3 mm plate chassis for 12 kg autonomous skid-steer robot, 450x400x350 mm envelope, 2 m/s max + 20 mm obstacle.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell (SHELL181) for plate, beam for motor mount studs

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: UK Robotics Challenge 2026 rulebook PDF not surfaced in catalog. Values inferred from typical IET mobile-robot benchmark and consistent with UK university mobile-robotics envelopes.

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Plate in dominantly bending; stress gate adequate.
